title: Florida Man
description: Inasmuch as Wild Things uses the humid deliria of South Florida to serve as a representative paradigm for the vulgar desperation of American life as a whole, so too does it presciently use Sam Lombardo to presage the “Florida Man” trope of the 21st century—maniacal, insanely confident men who make headlines by getting self-ensnared in bizarre, Rube Goldbergian schemes of their own making, men whose unflagging confidence in their own intelligence blinds them from their own blood-simple nai
hook: How a sleazy '90s thriller presaged the absurd, unstoppable archetype of the Florida Man—id unleashed by humidity and American desperation.

title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 is capitalist art that hates capitalist art
description: The Devil Wears Prada is one of the great millennial fairy tales.   Released in 2006, the year before the financial crisis and Great Recession would come for us all, the movie (based on a novel inspired by writer Lauren Weisberger’s experience working for Anna Wintour at Condé Nast) posits a subversive fantasy: Our heroine Andrea […]
hook: The Devil Wears Prada 2 is a capitalist film that genuinely hates capitalism, and that contradiction is exactly what makes it work.

title: Culture Schlock: Olivia Rutigliano Talks with David Denby About Betty Friedan, Mel Brooks, Norman Mailer, and Leonard Bernstein
description: In this episode, Olivia Rutigliano welcomes New Yorker writer David Denby on the show, to discuss his rave-reviewed new book, Eminent Jews, a study of four different Jewish artists (Betty Friedan, Mel Brooks, Norman Mailer, and Leonard Bernstein). Listen here:
hook: David Denby on four Jewish artists—Friedan, Brooks, Mailer, Bernstein—and how ambition, humor, and rage shaped American culture.
